title: Stockholm Boat Rental: How to Choose Your Vessel in 2026
html:
Deciding how to rent a boat in Stockholm starts with the archipelago, Djurgården harbour, and a realistic daily budget of €200 to €800. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local owners so you can match the right vessel to your plans.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Stockholm?

A shared island tour costs from €50 per person; a private motorboat starts around €250 for a half-day. Prices vary by boat size, age, season, and whether a skipper is included. Always check the full listing for fuel and cleaning fees. BoatHire24 shows transparent breakdowns per vessel. The platform currency is EUR, so what you see is what you pay. For a full day on a premium boat like a Fairline 22, expect €400 to €700. To compare verified boat listings, rent a boat in Stockholm through BoatHire24 and filter by price and features.

Where to set off in Stockholm

The city waterfront offers several excellent departure points, from central harbours to island marinas. Start from Nybroviken or Strandvägen for the quickest route into the inner archipelago. Riddarfjärden gives you a broad Lake Mälaren view before you reach salt water. For southward trips, Saltsjöbaden marina provides easy access to the outer islands. North of the city, Vaxholm harbour is a popular base for exploring the Stockholm Archipelago. Fjäderholmarna, just an hour’s gentle cruise from the centre, makes a perfect first stop.

Which boat to rent

Choose the right boat type based on group size and activity.
  1. Motorboat (e.g., Olympic 490), Best for small groups of 3, 4 people wanting a fast, flexible island hop. From €250 per day.
  2. Bowrider (e.g., Axopar 22), Ideal for 5, 6 passengers for coastal exploring and sunbathing. From €350 per day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g., Lagoon 42), Spacious for 8, 10 guests on a full-day sail around Utö. From €600 per day.
  4. Cabin cruiser (e.g., Fairline 28), Good for a couple or family with overnight stays in the outer islands. From €450 per day.
  5. Rigid inflatable (RIB), Perfect for 4, 6 adventurers who want speed and thrills. From €300 per half-day.

For detailed specs on one popular model, you can review this speedboat on BoatHire24.

Cost breakdown by rental style

Expect a clear difference between budget tours and premium charters.
  • Shared island tour (3 hours, up to 12 guests): from €50 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, 4, 5 passengers): from €250.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, 8, 10 guests): from €600.
  • Crewed yacht full-day (6, 8 guests, with skipper): from €800.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Stockholm?

Sweden does not require a formal licence for recreational boats under 10 metres, but the final decision rests with the owner. Many charter operators ask for proof of experience or a short briefing. Some larger yachts may require a Swedish boating licence or equivalent. The operator confirms exact requirements when you book through BoatHire24. Be prepared to show a driving licence or previous boating certificate. In July, the prevailing south-westerly wind (SV) can pick up, so a calm morning is best for novice skippers.
If you are new to Stockholm waters, book an operator who includes a quick familiarisation run around Skeppsholmen before you head into the archipelago.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50, €800 per day
  • Peak months: June, August
  • Trip length: 4 hours (half-day) or 8 hours (full-day)
  • Capacity: 2, 12 passengers depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR
  • Key departure: Nybroviken, Stockholm

Questions about boat rental in Stockholm

Can I rent a boat in Stockholm without a licence?

Many smaller boats up to 10 metres can be hired without a formal licence, but the owner sets the policy so check each listing carefully.

Is it safe to boat in the Stockholm Archipelago?

Yes, the archipelago is well-marked and sheltered, but you should always check the weather forecast and carry a basic chart or GPS app.

What is the best month for boating in Stockholm?

July offers the warmest weather and longest daylight hours, with water temperatures reaching 18°C and reliable sunshine for island hopping.

How far can I go in a half-day trip from central Stockholm?

A typical half-day cruise covers about 10, 15 nautical miles round trip, enough to reach Fjäderholmarna and circle back with time for a coffee stop.

Whether you are planning a family outing or a solo adventure, browse the listings on BoatHire24 to find your ideal boat in Stockholm. If you own a boat and want to earn extra income, you can list your vessel on the same marketplace and reach travellers from around the world. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Stockholm or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Stockholm and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
